input.instruction: A weakener fired on a credibility evidence package. Using ONLY the supplied pattern definition and measures, explain what the flag means, why it fired, and what the engineer should do. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge: report on the evidence package, never on whether the model is correct.

fired_pattern.definition: A declared problem symmetry the solution must respect is broken by the surrogate.

case_context.cou: surrogate predicts peak stress for a structural-margin decision
case_context.decision_consequence: structural margin on the predicted field

answer_key.gold_mechanism: The problem is mirror-symmetric so the true response must be symmetric; an 18% predicted asymmetry across the symmetry plane is a model-form defect a good aggregate NRMSE hides.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

parameters.blocks: use for the margin until explained

forbidden_claims: asserts the peak-stress value is wrong (a verdict beyond the symmetry violation)
treats the good NRMSE as evidence the symmetry holds

hardness.obvious_signal: the displacement-field fit to the reference is excellent (NRMSE 1.1%)
hardness.conflicting_signal: the loading and geometry are mirror-symmetric, so the response must be symmetric, but the surrogate's predicted field is asymmetric by 18% across the symmetry plane — it breaks a symmetry the physics guarantees

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 40 model-form: a solution that breaks a symmetry the problem guarantees is inadequate regardless of aggregate fit.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
